tool_args.label: Subway Guideline Revisions Staff Summary

payload.title: Subway Guideline Revisions
payload.description: Staff summary to the Board of technical clarifications to the Rapid Transit Loading and Frequency Guidelines

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Sarah Wyss, Acting Chief, Operations Planning
tool_args.raw_text: Sarah Wyss, Acting Chief, Operations Planning

tool_args.label: MTA Board
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA Board-approved Rapid Transit Loading Guidelines

tool_args.label: MTA New York City Transit
tool_args.raw_text: New York City Transit

tool_args.label: Allyson Bechtel, Project Manager
tool_args.raw_text: Project Manager Name Allyson Bechtel

tool_args.label: Loading guidelines modified to remove seat-based references
tool_args.raw_text: The current Loading Guidelines, which refer to specific numbers of seats per car, must be modified to refer only to the total numbers of passengers per car, while keeping the Guideline loads by time of day unchanged.

payload.claim_text: The current Loading Guidelines, which refer to specific numbers of seats per car, must be modified to refer only to the total numbers of passengers per car, while keeping the Guideline loads by time of day unchanged.
payload.subject: Rapid Transit Loading Guidelines

tool_args.label: Multiple route designations on same schedule treated as single route
tool_args.raw_text: The Guidelines need to be clarified on pages H-22 through H-24A to reflect current, long-standing practices, namely to specify that services scheduled together on the same schedule with separate route designations, including but not limited to the (6)<6>, (7)<7>, (F)<F>, (N)(W) and (J)(Z), should be treated as single routes in the Guidelines

payload.claim_text: Services scheduled together on the same schedule with separate route designations, including but not limited to the (6)<6>, (7)<7>, (F)<F>, (N)(W) and (J)(Z), should be treated as single routes in the Guidelines
payload.subject: Rapid Transit Loading Guidelines

tool_args.label: Guidelines apply except when operationally infeasible
tool_args.raw_text: The Guidelines also require a technical clarification to note that they apply except when operationally infeasible. This is the current, long-standing practice.

payload.claim_text: The Guidelines also require a technical clarification to note that they apply except when operationally infeasible. This is the current, long-standing practice.
payload.subject: Rapid Transit Loading Guidelines

tool_args.label: No changes to current subway service frequencies
tool_args.raw_text: These Guideline revisions will not lead to changes in current subway service frequencies.

payload.claim_text: These Guideline revisions will not lead to changes in current subway service frequencies.
payload.subject: Rapid Transit Loading Guidelines

tool_args.label: R211 cars average 30 seats per car
tool_args.raw_text: The new R211's have an average of 30 seats per car

tool_args.label: Prior B Division cars (R143, R160, R179) average 42 seats per car
tool_args.raw_text: Prior to the R211's, the most recently delivered B Division cars (R143, R160, and R179) have an average of 42 seats per car

payload.scope: R143, R160, R179 B Division cars

tool_args.label: R211 off-peak load 37 vs 53 for prior cars under existing guideline
tool_args.raw_text: applying the current 125% of a seated load Off-Peak Guideline to the R211 would yield an Off-Peak load of 37 passengers per car, which is a 30% reduction from that of the R143, R160, and R179 B Division cars

tool_args.label: Prior B Division off-peak guideline load 53 passengers per car
tool_args.raw_text: Prior to the R211's, the most recently delivered B Division cars (R143, R160, and R179) have an average of 42 seats per car, translating into an Off-Peak Guideline load of 53 passengers per car

payload.scope: R143, R160, R179 B Division cars

tool_args.label: R211 off-peak load 30% reduction vs prior cars
tool_args.raw_text: applying the current 125% of a seated load Off-Peak Guideline to the R211 would yield an Off-Peak load of 37 passengers per car, which is a 30% reduction from that of the R143, R160, and R179 B Division cars

payload.scope: R211 vs R143/R160/R179 B Division cars

tool_args.label: A Division 51'4" cars peak max guideline load 110 riders per car
tool_args.raw_text: A Division - 51'4\" Cars: Current and Proposed Peak Max Guideline Load/Car 110

tool_args.label: B Division 60'6" cars peak max guideline load 145 riders per car
tool_args.raw_text: B Division - 60'6\" Cars: Current and Proposed Peak Max Guideline Load/Car 145

tool_args.label: B Division 75' cars peak max guideline load 175 riders per car
tool_args.raw_text: B Division - 75' Cars: Current and Proposed Peak Max Guideline Load/Car 175

tool_args.label: A Division proposed off-peak load 50 passengers per car
tool_args.raw_text: A Division - 51'4\" Cars: Proposed Off-Peak Load/Car (Rounded to Nearest 5) 50

tool_args.label: B Division 60'6" proposed off-peak load 55 passengers per car
tool_args.raw_text: B Division - 60'6\" Cars: Proposed Off-Peak Load/Car (Rounded to Nearest 5) 55

tool_args.label: B Division 75' proposed off-peak load 90 passengers per car
tool_args.raw_text: B Division - 75' Cars: Proposed Off-Peak Load/Car (Rounded to Nearest 5) 90

tool_args.label: R211 cars have fewer seats but improved circulation and standing arrangements
tool_args.raw_text: The recently introduced R211 B Division cars have fewer seats per car than older B Division car classes while also offering improved circulation and better arrangements for standing and leaning.

payload.claim_text: The recently introduced R211 B Division cars have fewer seats per car than older B Division car classes while also offering improved circulation and better arrangements for standing and leaning.
payload.subject: R211 B Division cars

tool_args.label: B Division lines with off-peak loads within guidelines would exceed with R211s
tool_args.raw_text: B Division lines that currently carry off-peak loads within or at Guidelines, including the (A)(C)(D)(E)(F)(G)(L)(N)(Q)(R), would, if converted to R211's, end up with loads above the 125% of seated load Guideline level, even if ridership has not changed.

payload.claim_text: B Division lines that currently carry off-peak loads within or at Guidelines, including the (A)(C)(D)(E)(F)(G)(L)(N)(Q)(R), would, if converted to R211's, end up with loads above the 125% of seated load Guideline level, even if ridership has not changed.
payload.subject: R211 cars

warnings: claim.routes is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route arrays should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

tool_args.label: Physical limitations, maintenance, and construction may make guidelines infeasible
tool_args.raw_text: Additional constraints, including but not limited to maintenance and construction work in the subway, may make it operationally infeasible to apply the loading guidelines or policy headways.

payload.claim_text: Additional constraints, including but not limited to maintenance and construction work in the subway, may make it operationally infeasible to apply the loading guidelines or policy headways.
payload.subject: Rapid Transit Loading Guidelines

tool_args.label: Current off-peak loads are 46% of peak max on A Division and 37% on B Division
tool_args.raw_text: The current Off-Peak Guideline loads represent 46% of the maximum Peak Guideline load on the A Division and 37% of the maximum Peak Guideline load on the prior B Division cars.

payload.claim_text: The current Off-Peak Guideline loads represent 46% of the maximum Peak Guideline load on the A Division and 37% of the maximum Peak Guideline load on the prior B Division cars.
payload.subject: Rapid Transit Loading Guidelines

tool_args.label: Branch services may have greater-than-policy headways if combined service at peak load point is within policy
tool_args.raw_text: If through service on a subway line splits to serve more than one branch, and average passenger loads are within Loading Guideline levels, then the headway on each branch may be greater than the Policy Headway as long as the combined service is consistent with the Policy Headway at the peak load point on the line.

payload.claim_text: If through service on a subway line splits to serve more than one branch, and average passenger loads are within Loading Guideline levels, then the headway on each branch may be greater than the Policy Headway as long as the combined service is consistent with the Policy Headway at the peak load point on the line.
payload.subject: Rapid Transit Frequency Guidelines

tool_args.label: Policy headways defined as 10-12 min normally, 20 min overnight
tool_args.raw_text: MTA policy is to ensure reasonably frequent subway service (defined as 10- to 12-minute headways) at all times of day, except the overnight period, when policy headways are 20 minutes.

payload.claim_text: MTA policy is to ensure reasonably frequent subway service (defined as 10- to 12-minute headways) at all times of day, except the overnight period, when policy headways are 20 minutes.
payload.subject: Rapid Transit Frequency Guidelines

tool_args.label: A Division weekday peak 4-min headway: 8,250 riders per half hour
tool_args.raw_text: A Division 51'4\" car: Weekday Peak 4 min headway, Load/Car 110, Cars/Train 10, Trips per 1/2 Hour 7.5, Riders per 1/2 Hour 8,250

tool_args.label: A Division weekday peak 2-min headway: 16,500 riders per half hour
tool_args.raw_text: A Division 51'4\" car: Weekday Peak 2 min headway, Load/Car 110, Cars/Train 10, Trips per 1/2 Hour 15, Riders per 1/2 Hour 16,500

tool_args.label: B Division 60'6" weekday peak 2-min headway: 21,750 riders per half hour
tool_args.raw_text: B Division 60'6\" car: Weekday Peak 2 min headway, Load/Car 145, Cars/Train 10, Trips per 1/2 Hour 15, Riders per 1/2 Hour 21,750

tool_args.label: B Division 75' weekday peak 2-min headway: 21,000 riders per half hour
tool_args.raw_text: B Division 75' car: Weekday Peak 2 min headway, Load/Car 175, Cars/Train 8, Trips per 1/2 Hour 15, Riders per 1/2 Hour 21,000

tool_args.label: Passenger loads rounded to nearest five riders reflecting measurement precision
tool_args.raw_text: Rounding the number of riders to the nearest five reflects the precision with which passenger loads can be measured; the margin of error in such measurements is plus or minus five riders.

payload.claim_text: Rounding the number of riders to the nearest five reflects the precision with which passenger loads can be measured; the margin of error in such measurements is plus or minus five riders.
